campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council debated the matter before making a decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases for fiscal year 2026
Finance department presentation on tax policy changes
Council deliberation and voting on the new rates
Emergency preparedness coordination mentioned

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or policy. After listening to residents, they decided to move forward with the proposed ordinance and scheduled it for final approval at their next meeting rather than going through the standard multi-step reading process.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on proposed ordinance
Council waived first reading requirement to expedite the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda highlights provided

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve official meeting records from their previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign several construction and professional service contracts, including work with QLM, Inc., and approved a resolution related to town improvements.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved resolution for town infrastructure improvements
Authorized three-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agendas for May 5 and May 12, 2026. The council reviewed what items should be added to these meeting agendas, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked that proper public notice requirements were followed for agenda items.
keyTopics: May 5, 2026 final agenda - adding new items and setting discussion times
May 12, 2026 draft agenda - planning future council business
Public records review for April 16-23, 2026
Notice and waiver requirements for council agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on April 30, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, hear public input from residents, and discuss grant funding for neighborhood projects in the upcoming fiscal year. The council reviewed recommendations for which neighborhood grant requests should receive funding and discussed attendance at neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant applications and funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Meeting minutes approval
Neighborhood meeting attendance and participation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to provide public comments both in person and online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online)
Special study session meeting

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 30, 2026 to approve meeting notes from two previous Economic Development Committee sessions held in January and March. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about any economic development topics for up to 3 minutes each.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period on economic development matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The city council met to discuss putting up large promotional signs around Levi's Stadium for the 2026 FIFA World Cup events, and to approve a new labor agreement with Santa Clara firefighters that will run through 2029. One item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New firefighter labor contract through 2029
Staff withdrawal of an agenda item

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held hearings on several financial and planning matters for the upcoming fiscal year 2026-27, including tax policies and development fees. The council also received a presentation about Santa Clara County's plan to address homelessness in the community.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 development fees and user charges
County homelessness prevention plan presentation
Approval of meeting minutes and calendar updates

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on April 29, 2026 to discuss a proposal for the Old Trace Road property. The main item was a developer's request to split a vacant 1-acre lot into 9 separate lots so they can build 9 new single-family homes and 7 smaller accessory dwelling units (like in-law apartments), along with one private street to connect them.
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1.02-acre vacant lot into 9 residential lots at 4103 Old Trace Road
Proposal to build 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller rental units)
Project is exempt from full environmental review under state law (AB 130)
Property is zoned for residential estate use
Council elections for Chair and Vice Chair positions
